keyEvents.impact: Birth trauma of communal violence; permanent rivalry with Pakistan over Kashmir

keyEvents.event: Defeat in border war with China
keyEvents.impact: Lasting distrust of Beijing and focus on Himalayan border defense

keyEvents.event: Victory over Pakistan and creation of Bangladesh
keyEvents.impact: Confirmed regional military dominance in South Asia
Pursuit of strategic autonomy outside the non-proliferation order
Shift from socialist autarky to rapid market-driven growth and global integration
Open nuclear deterrent against both Pakistan and China
Hardened anti-China posture; deepened Quad cooperation with US, Japan, Australia

history.narrative: A civilizational state determined to rise on its own terms, guarding strategic autonomy by balancing among all great powers rather than joining blocs. Restrained but unyielding on Kashmir and the Chinese border, it converts demographic weight and rapid growth into great power claims.

redLines.description: Major Pakistan-sponsored terrorist attack on Indian soil
Chinese attempts to alter the Line of Actual Control by force
Nuclear first use or nuclear blackmail against India
Blockade of Indian Ocean sea lanes vital to energy imports

goals.description: Sustain rapid growth and become the world's third-largest economy
Deter Pakistan and eliminate cross-border terrorism
Counter Chinese encirclement on the Himalayan border and in the Indian Ocean
Win recognition as a great power, including a permanent UN Security Council seat
Build domestic defense and manufacturing capacity to cut import dependence
